mainText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Saturday 23 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products

- Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026 this week, claiming it outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro across almost all benchmarks while running four times faster, making it the headline engine for agentic workflows.

- Google also introduced Managed Agents in the Gemini API, letting developers spin up a reasoning, tool-using agent in an isolated Linux environment with a single API call - a meaningful shortcut for anyone prototyping agentic products.

- Subquadratic's SubQ 1M-Preview became the first commercially available LLM built on a non-transformer architecture, shipping with a native 12 million token context window and claiming around one fifth the cost of frontier models.

- DeepSeek V4-Pro, released under the MIT licence, scores 80.6 percent on SWE-bench Verified at just $0.27 per million input tokens, versus $5 for Claude Opus 4.7 and $15 for GPT-5.5 - a pricing gap that resets the build-versus-buy maths for any production coding workload.

- On the new ClawBench evaluation of 153 real-world web tasks - booking, purchasing, applications - the best-performing frontier model, Claude Sonnet 4.6, only scored 33.3 percent, a useful reminder that agentic reliability on live sites remains an unsolved problem.

💷 UK personal finance

- The Bank of England held Bank Rate at 3.75% in April, with one MPC member voting for a hike to 4%, meaning savers can still lock in competitive rates before any further shift — the next MPC decision is 18 June.

- From April 2027, under-65s will only be able to save a maximum of £12,000 into cash ISAs each year, so maxing your full £20,000 allowance this tax year is worth prioritising now.

- Trading 212 leads easy-access cash ISAs at 4.62% AER, while Vanquis Bank pays 4.66% for a one-year fixed ISA and Secure Trust Bank pays 4.72% for two years.

- Chase's Saver With Boosted Rate offers 4.50% AER, though that headline figure includes a sizeable 2.23% bonus for 12 months, so the revert rate is worth checking before committing.

- Nationwide has cut rates on two-, three- and five-year fixed mortgages for first-time buyers and home movers by up to 0.25 percentage points, welcome news if you are approaching a remortgage.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics

- The coalition's Knesset dissolution bill passed its preliminary reading on 20 May with a massive 110 votes in favour and none against, meaning elections are now moving forward from the 27 October deadline.

- The crisis was triggered after Netanyahu admitted the Haredi draft bill lacked coalition support, prompting Degel HaTorah's spiritual leader to write to faction members that "we no longer have trust in Netanyahu."

- Former prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid have merged their parties Bennett 2026 and There is a Future into a new joint list called Together, with Bennett as leader.

- Israel has told UN envoy Nickolay Mladenov it will not withdraw from the so-called yellow line in Gaza, with a security source warning the IDF will restart fighting if Hamas refuses to disarm.

- Israel announced it killed Izz al-Din al-Haddad, leader of Hamas's military wing and an architect of the October 7 attacks, in a strike in Gaza City.

🇬🇧 UK politics

- In the 8 May local elections, Nigel Farage's Reform UK gained more than 600 council seats while Labour lost more than 450, suggesting Reform is now a credible governing threat rather than a protest movement.

- Farage celebrated Reform's first London council win in Havering, while in Hackney the Green Party's Zoe Garbett ousted the Labour incumbent mayor, meaning Labour is being squeezed from both flanks in the capital.

- Reform's internal polling, based on those results, projects the party on course to unseat over 110 Labour MPs at the next general election, including Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson, putting the schools brief itself at direct electoral risk.

- The government has announced around 4 billion pounds of national investment to expand SEND provision in the state sector, though delivery credibility depends on a Labour government that is visibly weakening.

- Labour's national poll rating now stands at just 18 percent, behind Reform at 25 percent, according to YouGov, and Starmer has publicly ruled out resigning, meaning pressure for a successor such as Wes Streeting is building but not yet resolved.

😴 Sleep and wearables

- Google rebranded the Fitbit app to Google Health on May 19 and launched the new Fitbit Air at 99 dollars with an AI coach built on Gemini, making it the first new Fitbit hardware since 2023 and a direct threat to your Whoop and Oura comparisons.

- The Fitbit Air is slimmer and lighter than Whoop at just 12 grams, while still promising up to a week of battery life, which could make it a compelling alternative if you ever consider moving on from your Fitbit data history.

- A Brigham and Women's Hospital study found the Oura Ring outperformed Apple Watch and Fitbit in four-stage sleep classification, scoring 5 percent higher than Apple Watch and 10 percent higher than Fitbit.

- Stanford Medicine's SleepFM AI model can use one night of physiological sleep recordings to predict risk across more than 100 health conditions, trained on nearly 600,000 hours of sleep data from 65,000 participants - relevant context for how your eight years of Fitbit data could one day be interrogated far more deeply.

- HRV is now considered a promising biomarker for detecting subtle autonomic changes in sleep disorders including REM Sleep Behavior Disorder, reinforcing why tracking overnight HRV trends in your Fitbit data is scientifically meaningful.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market

- SaaS valuations hit decade-plus lows in Q1 2026, with the SaaS Capital Index selling off sharply as markets priced AI in as an existential threat to the business model - meaning any fractional GTM pitch now needs to lead with efficiency, not just growth.

- Salesforce's 2026 State of Sales report, surveying over 4,000 professionals, found 54% of organisations are already deploying AI agents across the sales cycle, with top-performing teams 1.7x more likely to use them than underperformers.

- ICONIQ's 2026 State of Go-to-Market report records sales cycle compression from 25 weeks to 19 weeks year-on-year, while 48% of companies now report hybrid pricing as their primary model - signalling that fixed-seat deals are increasingly a harder sell.

- Battery Ventures data shows AI-native SaaS companies reach 100 million dollars ARR in 6.3 years versus 8 years for traditional SaaS, a 27% faster trajectory that is reshaping where investors are concentrating capital.

- The median seed round for US SaaS companies now sits at 3.1 million dollars, while median post-money seed valuations hit an all-time high of 24 million dollars in 2026, up from 18 million dollars a year earlier.

Today's question: What is the one small change you can make today that compounds across your work, money, and sleep?
